title: Sveltekit sass + icon-font config issues
description: fixes for issues encounted with sass + icon-font + sveltekit

body.raw: ## ![Futuristic House Concept](https://i.pinimg.com/enabled_hi/564x/8b/09/40/8b094052909b4402b845581c7a5d6d79.jpg) Synthbeat 86 - synthetic heartbeats by haze long | openSea ## dart sass deprecation warning log `vite.config.js(ts)` ```js import { defineConfig } from "vite"; import { sveltekit } from "@sveltejs/kit/vite"; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [sveltekit()], css: { preprocessorOptions: { scss: { api: "modern-compiler", // or "modern" }, }, }, }); ``` > this tells the compiler to ues the modern verion of the sass api alternatively the logs can be silenced with ```js import { defineConfig } from "vite"; import { sveltekit } from "@sveltejs/kit/vite"; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [sveltekit()], css: { preprocessorOptions: { scss: { silenceDeprecations: ["legacy-js-api"], }, }, }, }); ``` ### Deprecation after running build `install sass-migrator` ```bash npm i -g sass-migrator ``` `migrate the deprecated source` ```bash sass-migrator module node_modules/material-symbols/_core.scss ``` ## Preloading Fonts in svelte source: [fontsource](https://fontsource.org/); this project enables the use of locally hosted fonts from google which can be used to preload fonts example: sveltekit install font with desired package manager | pnpm in my case ```bash pnpm install @fontsource/ ``` usage ```css import "@fontsource/open-sans/500.css"; // Weight 500. import "@fontsource/open-sans/900-italic.css"; // Italic variant. ``` or ```css import "@fontsource/open-sans"; // defaults to normal (weight: 400). ``` if using sass. just prepend the `~` to the import path. this enables vite (recommended) to resolve the path of the font > 🥊 I foundout that this doesn't work in sass as the compiler still complains of missing font ie the path not resolved > however the official specs show that these are imported in the root layout. so in svelte this will be in '+layout.svelte' at the routes root `+layout.svelte` ```svelte ``` **usage** ```scss @import "~@fontsource/open-sans"; ``` depending on the use case, you can either install variable or static fonts which will enable you to use the varying font weights (variable) from a single source font install or by installing individual fonts weights of the same font (statis) ## How to preload google font icons via an npm source: [google material symbols](https://github.com/marella/material-symbols/tree/main/material-symbols#readme) source: [icon reference](https://marella.me/material-symbols/demo/#sharp) **install** ```bash pnpm install material-symbols@latest ``` `+layout.svelte` ```svelte face face face ``` > 💡 TIP | to reduce build size only import the type you'd use - rounded - outlined - sharp ```svelte ```
